title: Method for operating an active optical sensor system and active optical sensor system
abstract: For operating an active optical sensor system (3), it is determined whether a temperature of at least one light source of an emitter unit (11) fulfills a predefined operating condition. If the operating condition is fulfilled, the emitter unit (11) is controlled to output light (5a) into an outer environment of the active optical sensor system (3) with an optical output power, which has a predefined target value or lies within a predefined target range. If the operating condition is not fulfilled, the emitter unit (11) is controlled to output light (5a) into the environment with an optical output power, which is smaller than the target value or lies below the predefined target range.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . Method for operating an active optical sensor system (3) for a motor vehicle (1 ), which comprises an emitter unit (11 ) with at least one light source, wherein it is determined whether a temperature of the at least one light source fulfills a predefined operating condition; if the temperature fulfills the operating condition, the active optical sensor system (3) is operated according to a normal operation mode, wherein the emitter unit (11) is controlled to output light (5a) into an outer environment of the active optical sensor system (3) with an optical output power, which has a predefined target value or lies within a predefined target range; and if the temperature does not fulfil the operating condition, the active optical sensor system (3) is operated in a preliminary operating mode, wherein the emitter unit (11) is controlled to output light (5a) into the environment such that, if the temperature would fulfil the operating condition, an optical output power of the light (5) would be smaller than the target value or lie below the predefined target range. 2. Method according to according to claim 1 , wherein, during the preliminary operating mode, determining whether the temperature fulfills the operating condition is repeated and, if the temperature fulfills the operating condition according to the repeated determining, the active optical sensor system (3) is operated according to the normal operation mode; and if the temperature does not fulfil the operating condition according to the repeated determining, the active optical sensor system (3) remains to be operated according to the preliminary operation mode. 3. Method according to claim 1 , wherein, during the preliminary operating mode, determining whether the temperature fulfills the operating condition is repeated periodically until the temperature fulfills the operating condition and, when the 

 temperature fulfills the operating condition, the preliminary operating mode is ended and the active optical sensor system (3) is operated in the normal operating mode. 4. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the temperature fulfills the operating condition, only if the temperature is equal to or greater than a predefined minimum temperature and/or only if a rate of change of the temperature is equal to or less than a predefined maximum rate. 5. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein, during the preliminary operating mode, the at least one light source is heated by means of a heating device of the active optical sensor system (3). 6. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the emitter unit (11 ) is controlled to output the light (5a) into the environment as a plurality of light (5a) pulses; and a number of light (5a) pulses emitted into the environment during a predefined time period is smaller in the preliminary operation mode than in the normal operating mode. 7. Method according to one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the at least one light source comprises at least one laser diode, which is operated in a burst mode; and a number of micro-pulses per burst is smaller in the preliminary operation mode than in the normal operating mode. 8. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one light source is supplied with less electrical power in the preliminary operation mode than in the normal operating mode. 9. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein, during the preliminary operating mode, at least a part of light generated by the at least one light source is not emitted into the environment. 10. Method according to claim 9, wherein the part of the light, which is not emitted to the environment is directed to hit an inner region of a housing (12) of the active optical sensor system (3). 11 . Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one light source comprises at least one laser diode and, which is operated out of resonance during the preliminary operating mode. 12. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein determining whether the temperature fulfills the predefined operating condition comprises measuring the temperature by means of a temperature sensor. 13. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein determining whether the temperature fulfills the predefined operating condition comprises measuring an emission wavelength or an emission wavelength spectrum or an emission frequency or an emission frequency spectrum of the at least one light source. 14. Method according to one of claims 1 to 12, wherein determining whether the temperature fulfills the predefined operating condition comprises determining whether an emission wavelength of the at least one light source or a maximum wavelength of an emission wavelength spectrum of the at least one light source is greater than a predefined threshold wavelength; or determining whether an emission frequency of the at least one light source or a minimum frequency of an emission frequency spectrum of the at least one light source is smaller than a predefined threshold frequency. 15. Method according to claim 14, wherein light generated by the at least one light source is passed through a band-pass filter or through a low-pass filter of the active optical sensor system (3) and the threshold wavelength corresponds to an inverse lower cut-off frequency of the band-pass filter or the low-pass filter, respectively; or the threshold frequency corresponds to the lower cut-off frequency of the band-pass filter or the low-pass filter, respectively. 16. Method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein, during the normal operating mode, portions (5b) of the output light (5a), which are reflected by an object (4) in the outer environment of the active optical sensor system (3), are detected by a detector unit (9) of the active optical sensor system (3) and a distance of the object (4) from the active optical sensor system (3) is determined based on the detected portions (5b). 17. Method for guiding a motor vehicle (1) at least in part automatically, wherein a method according to claim 16 is carried out and at least one control signal for guiding the motor vehicle (1 ) at least in part automatically is generated depending on the distance. 18. Active optical sensor system (3) for a motor vehicle (1 ), which comprises at least one control unit (6, 10) and an emitter unit (11 ) with at least one light source, wherein the at least one control unit (6, 10) is configured to determine whether a temperature of the at least one light source fulfills a predefined operating condition; if the temperature fulfills the operating condition, operate the active optical sensor system (3) in a normal operating mode and, in the normal operating mode, control the emitter unit (11 ) to output light (5a) into an outer environment of the active optical sensor system (3) with an optical output power, which has a predefined target value or lies within a predefined target range; and if the temperature does not fulfil the operating condition, operate the active optical sensor system (3) in a preliminary operating mode and, in the preliminary operating mode, control the emitter unit (11) to output light (5a) into the environment such that, if the temperature would fulfil the operating condition, an optical output power of the light (5a) would be smaller than the target value or lie below the target range. 19. Active optical sensor system (3) according to claim 18, wherein the at least one light source comprises at least one laser diode and/or the active optical sensor system (3) is a lidar sensor system. 20. Electronic vehicle guidance system (2) for a motor vehicle (1 ), which comprises an active optical sensor system (3) according to one of claims 17 or 18, wherein the active optical sensor system (3) comprises a detector unit (9), which is configured to 

detect, during the normal operating mode, portions (5b) of the output light (5a), which are reflected by an object (4) in the outer environment of the active optical sensor system (3), and the at least one control unit (6, 10) is configured to determine a distance of the object (4) from the active optical sensor system (3) based on the detected portions (5b); and to generate at least one control signal for guiding the motor vehicle (1 ) at least in part automatically depending on the distance.
